content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Project Template for Debugging
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Master the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to debug faster and ship high-quality client projects with ease.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template streamlines your architecture by enforcing modularity and automated error-handling patterns. By pairing this with Claude Code, you offload the cognitive load of debugging. This combination transforms chaotic codebases into predictable, maintainable systems, allowing you to resolve complex bugs in minutes rather than hours.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a wall of red console text at 3:00 AM, the client deadline is breathing down your neck, and the logic you wrote yesterday has seemingly unraveled into a web of impossible dependencies. Every minute you spend manually tracing variables is a minute your profitability—and reputation—erodes. 

But what if your codebase was designed to be self-healing? By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you move beyond 'writing code' and start engineering resilient, observable systems. It is time to stop fighting your own logic and start using advanced AI-driven architecture to do the heavy lifting for you.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure observability. This template forces a clean separation between business logic and side effects, making debugging trivial.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize an Antigravity project structure with strict modularity, TypeScript enforcement, and automated error-boundary logging.

steps.title: Inject Automated Error Handlers
steps.description: Create global error wrappers that automatically capture state context when a failure occurs, preventing 'silent' bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a global error-boundary decorator that captures stack traces, variable state, and input parameters upon exception.

steps.title: Context-Aware Debugging with Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a delta analysis between your broken function and the healthy Antigravity architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current file against the Antigravity design patterns. Identify the logic drift causing the runtime exception.

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Generate regression tests for the fixed bug to ensure the issue never resurfaces in future client iterations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate comprehensive unit tests for the resolved function using the Antigravity testing suite patterns.

steps.title: Refactor for Performance and Scalability
steps.description: Finalize the codebase by optimizing the resolved path according to project standards for long-term maintainability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the logic for optimal performance while adhering to the established Antigravity style guide.

pro_tips: Always define a 'source of truth' schema in your Antigravity config; it prevents 90% of data-type related bugs.
Use Claude Code's 'diff' mode to review AI-suggested fixes line-by-line; never blindly accept massive refactors.
Implement 'Telemetry-as-Code' by piping Antigravity error logs directly into your development console.
Keep your prompt library version-controlled within the project root to ensure consistent AI behavior across team members.
Run 'claude audit' every time you finish a new module to identify architectural debt before it becomes a blocking bug.
content.next_steps: After completing this setup, integrate your Antigravity project with your CI/CD pipeline to automate the testing phase, ensuring that every push is verified against your new, bug-resistant architecture.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours chasing elusive bugs and start shipping robust, high-performance software that your clients will love. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
